one_line_positioning: AI product and consulting professional lacking hands-on Python and agentic framework experience required for this development role.
narrative_angle: Candidate offers unique consulting experience and an AI product launch track record, but does not meet the core technical requirements (4+ years Python, agentic design).
lead_strengths: Led AI product from zero to 451 users in 7 months
Scoped AI data platform across 80+ bank departments
Architected emotionally intelligent AI system with behavioral science grounding
proof_points_to_emphasize: Co-developed RFP for AI-enabled data platform with LLM use cases
things_to_downplay: No Python development experience (listed as skill only, no projects)
No AI agent design or implementation experience
No agentic framework usage (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen)
No hands-on Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, cloud experience
Only 1 year total experience, likely not enough for 4+ years Python demand
tone_guidance: Acknowledge the skills mismatch honestly; do not attempt to reframe as a match.
recruiter_first_impression_target: This candidate is not a fit for a hands-on Python developer role; they are a product manager/consultant.
hiring_manager_objection: Lacks all core technical requirements: 4+ years Python, AI agent design, agentic frameworks, CI/CD, etc.
objection_preempt: Despite strong strategic AI product experience, the candidate does not have the hands-on development skills this role demands.
reasoning: The candidate's differentiator is strategic consulting and AI product management, but the role requires a hands-on developer with extensive Python and agentic framework expertise, which is absent from their evidence. All must_haves are gaps, making this a clear SKIP.
